agentApp.lab.title: Agent App Lab
agentApp.lab.description: The Lab connects Agent App package review, cache verification, installed state, setup, permissions, launch, App Manager, and cleanup preview without entering the production path.
agentApp.lab.package.title: Package Preview
agentApp.lab.package.description: The Lab reads the local fixture, computes package and manifest identity, then lets the P15 install flow verify the cache.
agentApp.lab.entries.title: Entry Projection
agentApp.lab.entries.description: Entries render as lab-only projections and do not enter command, Skill Catalog, or Workspace routes.
agentApp.lab.run.title: Mock Capability Host
agentApp.lab.run.description: P1 runs only the built-in mock action to validate the SDK facade, storage namespace, Artifact, and Evidence provenance. It does not execute App UI or workers.
agentApp.lab.run.adapterDescription: P2 validates the real adapter boundary for storage, Artifact, and Evidence through a local adapter store. It still does not execute App UI or workers.
agentApp.lab.run.empty: After a capability host is enabled, click any entry to generate a traceable experimental artifact.
agentApp.lab.run.success: Mock entry completed. Artifact and Evidence both carry agent_app provenance.
agentApp.lab.uiRuntime.title: UI Extension Host
agentApp.lab.uiRuntime.description: P3 mounts only a controlled App UI container inside the experiment island, injects a scoped SDK bridge, and does not register main routes or execute workers.
agentApp.lab.uiRuntime.empty: After UI runtime is enabled, click a page / panel / settings entry to verify the controlled UI container.
agentApp.lab.uiRuntime.mounted: UI entry mounted in the controlled container and still falls back to the Lab projection on failure.
agentApp.lab.contentDemo.title: APP Content Factory P4 Demo
agentApp.lab.contentDemo.description: Runs project creation, knowledge binding, content scenario planning, content assets, content-table Artifact, and Evidence through adapter capabilities.
agentApp.lab.contentDemo.empty: The P4 demo writes App namespace storage and generates a content_table Artifact with content_factory_demo Evidence.
agentApp.lab.contentDemo.completed: Content factory loop completed. Project, content scenarios, assets, Artifact, and Evidence all carry agent_app provenance.
agentApp.lab.capability.title: Capability Requirements
agentApp.lab.capability.description: Derived from requires, runtimePackage, storage, and entry declarations.
agentApp.lab.capability.name: Capability
agentApp.lab.readiness.title: Readiness Check
agentApp.lab.readiness.description: P15 explains host and setup gaps; the sample setup is Lab-only and never writes to a real workspace.
agentApp.lab.cleanup.title: Cleanup Dry-run
agentApp.lab.cleanup.description: P15 shows what failure, uninstall, or delete-data would clean without deleting non-Agent App data.
agentApp.lab.cleanup.empty: No cleanup targets in this category for P0.
agentApp.lab.boundary.title: Main Path Boundary
agentApp.lab.boundary.description: This Lab proves the App Host is removable without leaking experiment state into product paths.
agentApp.lab.boundary.noRuntime: By default, App UI bundles, workers and workflows do not run; P3/P4.2 only allow controlled UI Host and allow-listed workflow DSL.
agentApp.lab.boundary.noRegistry: Does not write to command, skill, artifact, or workspace global registries.
agentApp.lab.boundary.noStorage: Does not create real App storage namespaces; it only previews cleanup.
agentApp.lab.footer: The route is now at P16 Agent App Manager / Product Entry Gate; multi-app lifecycle, permission review, GUI smoke, and cleanup rehearsal are still required before a production entry.
agentApp.lab.workflowRuntime.enabledHint: P4.2 controlled workflow runtime is enabled: only allow-listed DSL steps run; raw worker bundles remain blocked.
agentApp.lab.workflowRuntime.completed: Controlled workflow completed with trace, policy and artifact refs preserved.
agentApp.lab.guard.title: Entry Runtime Guard
agentApp.lab.guard.description: P14 merges package verification, readiness, setup, and permission summaries before an entry runs or mounts.
agentApp.lab.guard.empty: Run or open a UI entry to see the P14 guard decision here.
agentApp.lab.guard.summary.allow: The guard allows this Lab-only entry to continue.
agentApp.lab.guard.summary.needs-setup: Required setup is missing, so the guard stopped this entry.
agentApp.lab.guard.summary.blocked: Package, readiness, or runtime policy blockers stopped this entry.
agentApp.lab.guard.summary.denied: Required permission was denied, so the entry will not run.
agentApp.lab.installFlow.title: Lab Install And Launch Flow
agentApp.lab.installFlow.description: P15 connects source, package review, verified cache, installed state, setup, permission prompt, launch, and cleanup into a Lab-only loop.
agentApp.lab.installFlow.setupResolved: The Lab sample setup resolves all required bindings.
agentApp.lab.installFlow.launchReady: Guard allows launch in the Lab.
agentApp.lab.manager.title: Agent App Manager
agentApp.lab.manager.description: P16 turns the single Lab flow into an experimental App management surface for installed apps, entry launcher, disable / uninstall, and cleanup evidence.
agentApp.lab.manager.launchDisabled.disa: The App is disabled and cannot launch entries
agentApp.lab.manager.launchDisabled.guar: Guard has not allowed launch or runtime is disabled
agentApp.lab.manager.evidence.empty: Lifecycle actions will create cleanup / launch evidence summaries here.
agentApp.lab.manager.evidence.noNonAppDa: Only Agent App namespaces are recorded; non-Agent App data is untouched.
agentApp.lab.manager.repositorySummary: P11 repository restored {{count}} apps with {{issues}} load issues

agentApp.apps.installReview.description: Confirm the app name, version, and available entries before installing.

agentChat.teamWorkbench.selectedTarget.d: This only locates targets in the workbench. It does not infer state from plain text or act on external tasks, reviews, or handoffs.
agentChat.teamWorkbench.reassignment.des: Selecting a target owner only fills an owner update instruction. After sending and execution, the backend result remains the source of truth.
agentChat.teamWorkbench.related.title: Related teammate chain
agentChat.teamWorkbench.related.descript: Matches the same target by member, task, work item, teammate record, and deliverable references. This only links existing state and does not create new state.
agentChat.teamWorkbench.transcript.title: Teammate record details
agentChat.teamWorkbench.transcript.descr: Content comes from the selected member activity preview. This only shows references and focus, and does not mix teammate output into the main reply.
agentChat.teamWorkbench.transcript.drill: This only shows existing queued inputs and history body. When no records exist, it does not synthesize waiting, tool, or message state.
agentChat.teamWorkbench.transcript.histo: Reading history body. The result is only used as teammate record preview and does not enter the main reply.
agentChat.teamWorkbench.transcript.noAct: No real-time activity has arrived for this task, and there is no history body to show yet. For full context, keep focusing the related task.
